title: Implement session DB polling: read messages table on timer
completion_summary: internal/poller package: modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go) opens /root/.hermes/state.db read-only with WAL hint; polls SELECT id, session_id, role, content, timestamp, tool_calls, tool_name FROM messages WHERE id > ? ORDER BY id on a configurable timer (--poll-interval, default 1s); tracks last-seen id in memory. Default streams only new messages (initial high-water mark = MAX(id)); --from-beginning replays existing rows for backfill / acceptance testing. Transient SQLite errors (locked/busy) are logged and retried, not fatal. Tested live: built host-side, scp'd to /usr/local/bin/observer in the VM, ran with --from-beginning and saw all 13 messages currently in state.db — including the foundation-06 skill_manage cycle (id 7 assistant tool_calls_len=252 → id 8 tool content_len=1845 → ids 9-13). Deliverables: observer/internal/poller/{message,poller}.go.
acceptance_criteria: Service polls messages table on configurable timer
Extracts (session_id, role, content, timestamp, tool_calls) from new rows
Service logs new messages as they appear in Hermes DB
